title: 30 Días
author: Cambia de hábitos, cambia de vida:Greg S. Reid

chapters.title: Commitment and discipline work for you
chapters.text:
Discipline and commitment are the keys to success. These qualities both propel you forward and protect you, pushing you to new heights while preventing serious falls. Self-control means carrying out your responsibilities even when you don't feel like it. Like muscles, the more you exercise discipline, the stronger it becomes.

It's easy to blame external circumstances when your discipline falters, but the harsh reality is that you alone are accountable for your life. It's not your boss, your partner, or the weather. Take responsibility for what you can control, and let go of what you can't.

Self-discipline grows when you understand the value of commitment. Keep your word to others, but especially to yourself. Breaking promises to yourself will chip away at your self-esteem and derail efforts toward your goals, making them seem less worthwhile to pursue. Remember that a commitment is a decision, so only make the ones you want to keep.

Build discipline through small wins and celebrate your progress.

You can choose to be either a victim or the main character in your story. The victim mindset believes that every issue is always someone else's fault, and that the future holds some magical remedy destined to fix everything. In contrast, the main character learns from the past, takes action in the present, and recognizes that they can be part of the solution rather than the problem.

Your choices directly shape your life. Every decision has both benefits and drawbacks, but none are inherently wrong. When you make mistakes, learn from them, forgive yourself, and move forward. Use criticism as a tool for growth. For example, choose your food wisely and exercise more if you want to be healthier. When it comes to success, carefully evaluate who you spend time with and how you utilize your hours. Follow along and see how to change your life, one step at a time.

chapters.title: Overcoming the chaos that comes with change
chapters.text:
There is no greater virtue than perseverance — that strength found in those who see defeat as a setback rather than failure. If a plan doesn't work, adapt it. Make the necessary changes and keep trying. Perseverance isn't about sticking to a system that isn't working, but about staying focused on your goal while adjusting your strategies. You can strengthen your determination by:

1. Setting a clear goal and being passionate about achieving it.
2. Designing a plan and taking daily actions to make progress.
3. Resisting negative and discouraging influences.
4. Having a motivating support system with people who encourage you to keep going.

It took Thomas Edison nearly ten thousand attempts to invent the light bulb. Such unwavering determination is an example you can follow whenever you wonder whether to continue pursuing your goals. After all, the fear of failure kills more dreams than failing itself. Edison's mentality embraced defeat as part of the process toward success. His example clearly shows that every adversity holds a valuable lesson.

At some point, you might even become afraid of disappointment. But do you remember how, as a child, you learned to walk and talk with just a nudge of courage to push you forward? That same resilience still lives within you today, waiting for you to apply it to any obstacle you face.

I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work. ~ Thomas Edison

If you want to be successful, you need to step out of your comfort zone. Personal growth often happens when you feel the most uncomfortable. Dare to do what others avoid by going the extra mile and taking full responsibility for your actions. Many mistakenly believe they must make a drastic change to redirect their lives, only to end up easily overwhelmed and forced to revert to their old habits. Instead, tackle your goals gradually, beginning with tasks that don't require too much time and energy.

Acknowledging your achievements is a great way to reward yourself for stepping out of your comfort zone. It doesn't matter how small they seem. Whether preparing a delicious and healthy breakfast, making a new friend, or accomplishing something monumental like finishing your degree, every achievement is precious. And by celebrating your progress, you'll identify new opportunities and spark ideas that will energize your plans.

Those who celebrate their victories typically find it easier to set clear goals. Writing those aspirations down in a visible place is a good way to remain aware of them. This simple practice will help you arrange your priorities and stay focused.

Endurance and patience grow when you adjust your plans and move toward your goals, regardless of obstacles.

chapters.title: Time management
chapters.text:
It's a shared experience to feel that your work demands so much of you that you no longer have time for yourself. You might even wish that the day lasted longer. Unfortunately, everyone has the same twenty-four hours, and no one can "control" time. But what you can control is how you organize your priorities to eliminate activities that don't add value, such as watching television or spending time on social media.

It's essential to set clear rules about your availability. When you respect your own time, others will too. Those quick three-minute interruptions actually cost you ten minutes because you need an additional seven to refocus. And having twelve such interruptions throughout the day can steal two hours from you. Silence these distractions by turning off notifications and setting specific times to check emails and messaging apps.

Disorganization is another hidden time thief. Many people feel overwhelmed by cluttered desks and piles of papers. Studies even reveal that executives can waste between 30% and 50% of their time searching for relevant documents.

Being busy is not the same as being efficient. Having the messiest desk in the office does not mean you are the hardest worker. ~ Marc Reklau

If you want to organize your work calendar, check out these ideas:

1. Prioritize the most crucial tasks during the first fifteen minutes of your day: This method will help you to start with a clear focus. Before you end your workday, spend another fifteen minutes planning the following day.
2. Use your email inbox as a to-do list: Archive completed items and keep only pending issues visible.
3. Complete the task if it takes less than five minutes: This small habit will prevent you from accumulating unnecessary backlogs.
4. Invest the right amount of effort to get it right the first time: It will reduce the need for corrections and revisions, saving valuable time.

It is also essential to learn the craft of saying NO. Every time you do so, you're saying YES to yourself. Although it may seem difficult initially, especially if you worry about not spending enough time with others, setting boundaries is key to managing your time efficiently. Saying NO does not make you selfish — it makes you someone who values their well-being and productivity.

Those who face these fears won't regret what they didn't do but will proudly remember what they dared to try. Most fears are just imaginary scenarios the mind creates to prepare for events that likely won't happen. Ask yourself what the worst-case scenario could be, then honestly assess whether the risk is worth worrying about.

Not taking risks carries its own cost, namely stagnation and missed growth opportunities. Acknowledge your fears, but don't let them get the better of you. True growth, success, and transformation only happen outside your comfort zone. Dare to fail, learn from your mistakes, and move on.

Smart time management is all about setting clear priorities, eliminating distractions, and confidently saying NO.

chapters.title: Detoxifying life
chapters.text:
One of humanity's biggest weaknesses revolves around temperament. Every minor annoyance can drain us of energy. It can be something as small as dropping your fork while eating, banging your knee on furniture, or dealing with that coworker you just can't stand. Reklau recommends listing everything that irritates you and organizing it by issues you can address. This approach can help you focus on what's within your control, allowing you to keep moving toward your goals.

Minimizing these daily nuisances is essential for a balanced lifestyle, but your ability to maintain it depends on your sleep quality. Start your day as peacefully as possible by establishing a morning routine. For example, prepare a nutritious breakfast, meditate for ten to fifteen minutes, go for a stroll, or journal. In the evening, reflect on your day by asking what went right and wrong, plan for tomorrow, and listen to music that inspires you. Avoid consuming negative news or disturbing content before bedtime, as these will raise your heart rate, disrupt your sleep, and ultimately affect your performance the next day.

If you want to make a significant change, start by raising your standards. Decide what you'll no longer tolerate, whether that's mediocrity, procrastination, or disrespectful behavior that holds you back from becoming your best self. This upgraded mindset might include surrounding yourself with authentic people, being punctual, letting others finish speaking, or clearly communicating when something bothers you.

Maintaining high standards will not only boost your self-esteem but will also shape how others treat you. For instance, you can say, "I don't like when people speak to me like that." If the behavior continues, you can either stand your ground or walk away. When you respect your boundaries, your relationships with others naturally improve.

While you can't change what happened, you can learn from it and move forward. Cherish your memories, but don't let them control you. Only then can you embrace new opportunities and face future challenges with confidence.

Remember that happiness isn't a destination but an attitude you cultivate daily. Many adults feel dissatisfied, longing for the joy they felt when they were younger, but those days have passed. The essence of adulthood is learning how to find happiness without relying on external factors while enjoying the little things in life. Why not start right now? Close your eyes, relive a happy moment, and watch how your mood lifts immediately.

You can control your energy and mood by letting go of what you can't control and enjoying what you already have.

chapters.title: New day, new opportunities
chapters.text:
Multitasking is a seductive fallacy that lures in the easily distracted or the overwhelmed. It creates the illusion that working on several things at once will help you finish faster. Yet anyone who has tried knows this "solution" is worse than the problem. Splitting your attention prevents you from fully concentrating, resulting in subpar results even if you successfully complete everything.
Abandon the myth of doing several things simultaneously. Focus your energy on one task at a time and do it well. This approach will make you more efficient and leave you more satisfied with your work.

Live life to the fullest by eliminating distractions and focusing on what truly matters.

Now, more than ever, remembering to take a break and disconnect from constant stimuli is crucial. Being in nature is like a balm for the soul. Take a walk on the beach, sit in a park, or just spend a few days away from technology. These moments help you recharge and appreciate life's simple pleasures. After all, your mental health is priceless.

Don't wait for the "perfect" moment to do what you love or make meaningful changes. Too many people realize too late that they lived chasing fleeting pleasures rather than their true desires. Life is too short to delay pursuing your dreams. Learn something new, spend time with loved ones, and savor the little things. Each day offers an opportunity to move closer to what you want, so don't let excuses stop you. Start today rather than waiting for that coveted moment that may never arrive.

Did you know? Tara Kraft and Sarah Pressman, from the University of Kansas, conducted a study showing that smiling can alter your response to difficult situations. It can lower your heart rate and stress levels, helping you feel better if you're unhappy. It's a simple way of signaling to your brain that everything will be alright.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Looking at all these tips, you might not know where to begin. The answer is simple — start with the advice that speaks to your biggest concern. Remember that changing your lifestyle isn't an overnight process. It's a process that requires effort, discipline, and dedication.
That's why these tips offer the most benefit when practiced for 30 days. As time passes, you'll notice positive shifts that will make you proud of how far you've come. You'll be able to reminisce and celebrate the improvements you've made in areas of your life that needed meaningful change.

Try this

• Accept that things won't always go as planned: This reality isn't a reason to give up on your goals. Keep persevering to achieve what you set out to do.
• Be disciplined, but be kind to yourself if you have trouble being consistent: Changes that add value require time and lots of patience. After all, nothing truly worthwhile comes easily.

Read it till the end?
